name: Machine Learning
tagline: Learns patterns from data to make predictions and decisions
problem:
Hand-written rules break when environments are messy, high-dimensional, or partially observed. Many problems cannot be solved by enumerating if-then logic.

Machine learning reframes the problem: choose a model family and fit parameters from data so the system generalizes to new cases. The hard part is not training; it is avoiding false confidence, bias, and overfitting.

The remaining problem for Machine Learning is operational reliability. A reader needs more than a name and a historical note: they need to know what failure this capability prevents, what resources it consumes, what signs show that it is failing, and when it is safer to stop than to improvise. This entry therefore treats machine learning as a teachable system with inputs, inspection points, records, and limits rather than as a single trick.
overview:
A learning pipeline selects data, defines an objective (loss), and uses optimization to fit parameters. Performance is judged on held-out data and against baseline methods.

Real deployments require monitoring and iteration: data drifts, labels are noisy, and correlations fail under regime change. The Codex treats this as an engineering discipline, not a one-time training run.

In collapse recovery, machine learning must be repairable with local materials, teachable to more than one person, and documented well enough that the method survives injury, migration, or leadership change. In a space-settlement context, the same capability must account for closed-loop resources, contamination control, spare-part scarcity, power budgets, and the cost of mistakes in a sealed habitat. The practical minimum is a controlled trial, written checks, safe storage, and review before scale-up.

principles.name: Generalization
principles.explanation: The goal is performance on unseen data; training accuracy alone is not enough.

principles.name: Bias-variance tradeoff
principles.explanation: Model capacity affects underfitting vs overfitting; regularization and data volume shift the balance.

principles.name: Evaluation discipline
principles.explanation: Train/validation/test splits and error analysis prevent self-deception.

rawMaterials.name: Electric power
rawMaterials.purpose: Energy for computation, storage, and networking
earthLocations: Electrical grids globally; generators in remote sites
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Solar + batteries is the simplest early-space pathway; plan for tight energy budgets and thermal rejection.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Stable voltage regulation, grounding, and basic power-quality control.

rawMaterials.name: Semiconductor devices
rawMaterials.purpose: Transistors and ICs that perform switching, storage, and signal conditioning
earthLocations: Global semiconductor supply chains; packaged IC distributors
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Early colonies import packaged devices; long-term local manufacture requires clean chemistry and crystal growth capabilities.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: ESD-safe handling; correct packaging, heat sinking, and derating for temperature and radiation.

rawMaterials.name: Documentation and standards
rawMaterials.purpose: Make designs reproducible and debuggable across teams and time
earthLocations: Libraries, universities, standards bodies, archived web
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Mirror core references offline; maintain printed and digital copies with checksums and redundancy.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Versioning, change control, and clear measurement units and assumptions.

rawMaterials.name: Training data
rawMaterials.purpose: Examples that encode the target task
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Data is a scarce resource early off-world; simulate where possible and design experiments to generate labeled data safely.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Cleaning, labeling, privacy/safety review, and dataset versioning.

rawMaterials.name: Compute time
rawMaterials.purpose: Optimization over large datasets and model parameters
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Prefer smaller models and task-specific systems early; schedule training around power availability and thermal limits.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Thermal management, job scheduling, and checkpointing.

buildSteps.title: Define the task and metrics
buildSteps.description: Specify inputs/outputs and what success means. Choose metrics and baseline approaches.
buildSteps.warningNote: Bad metrics create models that optimize the wrong thing.

buildSteps.title: Collect and version data
buildSteps.description: Gather representative data; record provenance; split into train/validation/test sets.
buildSteps.warningNote: Leakage between splits produces fake performance.

buildSteps.title: Choose model and objective
buildSteps.description: Start simple; pick loss; add regularization. Document assumptions.
buildSteps.warningNote: More complexity without data and evaluation increases risk.

buildSteps.title: Train with monitoring
buildSteps.description: Track loss/metrics; detect overfitting; tune hyperparameters cautiously.
buildSteps.warningNote: If training is unstable, reduce learning rate or simplify.

buildSteps.title: Evaluate and stress-test
buildSteps.description: Run on held-out tests; do error analysis; test for distribution shift and edge cases.
buildSteps.warningNote: Do not deploy without understanding failures.

buildSteps.title: Deploy with feedback
buildSteps.description: Monitor drift; retrain responsibly; keep a rollback plan.

history.event: Early statistical learning and perceptron era.
Statistical learning theory and practical ML expand.
Deep learning and large datasets drive major capability jumps.

impact:
Machine learning automates pattern recognition and prediction across science, industry, and communication. It also concentrates power and risk: biased data and opaque models can cause real harm at scale.

For The Codex, ML is a late-tree capability that depends on stable computing infrastructure and disciplined measurement, but it can accelerate discovery when used responsibly.
